Even though I LOVE the crazy city life that comes with living in Tokyo, sometimes it’s nice to get out into nature & enjoy a different side of Japan. A few weeks ago we visited one of the oldest hot spring towns in Japan, Arima Onsen, and stayed at a ryokan called Arima Onsen Gekkoen to get the full traditional Japanese ryokan experience. 

This was both of our first times here and it was everything we wanted and more. Having Kobe beef brought straight to our room for dinner, starting the morning in our own private bath, and being surrounded by cherry blossoms. 

For about 94,600 JPY ($600 USD) / person a night, what do you guys think?
*prices vary depending on the season and plan*

Thank you @kobe_arimaspa_gekkoen for such a relaxing time :) 

#pr #gekkoen #japan #japantravel #wheninjapan

🍣 Starting at $25.99 for all-you-can-eat sushi, hotpot, AND seafood in the DMV

@umipremiumbuffet is not your average buffet. This place is station after station of food, and every single one is all-you-can-eat. Fresh sashimi cut right at the station, snow crab legs available all day, made-to-order ramen, and a full hotpot setup with your own pot at the table.

At dinner for $39.99 you unlock premium seafood like oysters and lobster tails. And yes, drinks are included. Bubble tea, signature drinks, sodas, all of it.

They also have a dessert bar with cakes, matcha treats, mochi desserts, and a signature mango jelly that people keep going back for.

🍣 Fresh sushi, sashimi, nigiri, and specialty rolls made daily
🦀 Snow crab legs available all day at lunch AND dinner
🍜 Made-to-order ramen station
♨️ Hotpot with 4 broth options and a DIY sauce bar
🧋 Bubble tea and signature drinks included
🍰 Dessert bar with mochi, matcha, and mango jelly
🥩 Hot line with blue crab, lamb ribs, pork belly, grilled salmon, and more
🥗 Cold appetizer section and salad bar for lighter eaters

Looking for a hidden gem in West Virginia for a weekend getaway from DC or Baltimore? 🇩🇪✨

The Bavarian Inn in Shepherdstown, West Virginia honestly feels like a hidden European village with river views, an infinity pool, incredible food, and one of the most relaxing stays we’ve had in West Virginia.

Plus, it’s close to Harpers Ferry if you’re looking to try the new inflatable water park at @harpersferrywaterpark or go river tubing, white water rafting, and zip lining at @harpersferryadventurecenter.

Although honestly… once you see the infinity pool and those mountain views, you may not want to leave the resort at all. 🤭

✨If you’re searching for:
• Hidden gems in West Virginia
• Romantic getaways in West Virginia
• Weekend trips from DC
• Places to stay near Harpers Ferry
• Unique hotels in West Virginia
• Relaxing East Coast mountain escapes

…this place is absolutely worth adding to your list.

✨ FYI: Ask for the renovated rooms. You’ll thank me later.

We’ve stayed here FOUR times now and yes… I lowkey didn’t want to share this spot. 😂

This is one of those places where I truly go offline, relax, and just live life for a little bit. You feel me?

📌Save this for your next West Virginia getaway and send it to someone who needs a peaceful escape ASAP. #BavarianInn #WestVirginia #ShepherdstownWV #HarpersFerry  #dcmom

✅👇 see more details on the phrases

1. nama biiru [draft beer]
　・nama = draft, biiru = beer
　・another common phrase is chu-nama or nama-chu which means medium-sized mug draft beer

2. bin biiru [bottle beer]
　・when you order bottle beer, they will always bring you a small glass or gurasu in Japanese
　・if you want multiple glass, say gurasu hitotsu = 1 glass, futatsu = 2, mittsu = 3, yottsu = 4

3. reishu [cold(sake)]
　・recommended for ginjo / daiginjo, junmai ginjo / junmai daiginjo, or nama-sake

4. jouon [room temp(sake)]
　・recommended for junmai, honjozo, yamahai, kimoto

5. atsukan [hot(sake)]
　・recommended for junmai, honjozo, yamahai, kimoto

6. ichigo [sake unit 180ml]
　・ichigo will be served in tokkuri = sake carafe / vessel
　・nowadays, many izakaya offer by the glass or gurasu in Japanese, usually around 100ml

7. osusume [recommendation]
　・it's hard to choose which sake to order, even for a local;  when in doubt, just ask for osusume

8. sashimi no moriawase [sashimi assorted plate]
　・izakaya will often have a sashimi plate of one type of fish but if you want to taste many different types of fish, ask for sashimi no moriawase. 

9. okaikei onegaishimasu [check please]
　・when you ask for the check, many izakaya will bring a complimentary cup of hot tea with the check

10. kanpai! [cheers!🍺]　
　・the most important phrase to make friends in Japan!

#MySakeMates, #JapaneseIzakaya, #JapaneseSake, #JapanesePhrases, #JapaneseWords

Japanese Title: 氷菓
English Title: Hyouka
Romanized: Hyōka
Format: TV Anime
Source: Novel Series (Classic Literature Club)
Author: Honobu Yonezawa
Studio: Kyoto Animation
Last Air Date: September 17, 2012
Episodes: 22 + 1 OVA
Genres: Mystery, Slice of Life, School

Plot:The story follows Houtarou Oreki, a quiet student who believes in conserving energy and avoiding unnecessary effort.
However, his peaceful life changes when the curious and energetic Eru Chitanda joins the Classic Literature Club.
Whenever Chitanda says her famous phrase “I’m curious!”, Oreki is pulled into solving strange mysteries around school.
Together with their friends, they uncover secrets hidden in everyday life.

@pekinggourmetinn has been the OG spot for peking duck in NoVA for almost 50 years 🦆 

The star of the show is their whole peking duck ($80) and comes with all the fixings to make the perfect wrap. Definitely pricey, but I enjoyed the experience and can honestly say this is the best duck I’ve had in the area. The portions are pretty generous and are meant to be enjoyed family-style. 

There are so many photos of famous guests who have dined with them over the years, so I see why this spot is a classic!

worth leaving the itinerary for 🍺🇯🇵

If you think Japan is only sake and highballs… this might change your mind.

From Tokyo to Kyoto, these spots weren’t planned — they were found — and every single one was worth the detour.

1️⃣ Tokyo Beer Lab — Harajuku / Shibuya
A creative mash-up of craft beer, music, and art. Not your typical brewery — this place has a personality, and the beer backs it up.

2️⃣ OL by Oslob Brewing Co. — Shibuya
An alleyway gem we stumbled into after dinner. Packed, buzzy, and seriously good beer. If you see a spot outside, take it.

3️⃣ Spring Valley Brewery — Kyoto
A post-temple search turned into a “let’s stay for one more.” Cozy, unique brews, and exactly what you want after a long day exploring.

Save this for your next Japan trip 🍻
And tell me — Tokyo or Kyoto for craft beer? 👀

#JapanTravel
#CraftBeerJapan
#BeerTravel
#JapanItinerary

Yes, I know we just covered noodles, but I was craving these 3-in-1 hand ripped noodz so we’re here at @chinxianstyle for Day 2️⃣2️⃣ of #100AsianRestaurantsInMaryland. 

Located inside @bengongteaus, this spot is a serious must try. They’re easily one of my fave asian places in @visithocomd, and their food’s ridiculously good. I typically only get these two dishes, but I’m sure their other eats are tasty as well, lol. 

▪️3 in 1 Hand Ripped Noodles ($15.96): super yum biangbiang noodz topped with spicy hot oil, zha jiang, and tomato egg. Make sure you mix everything super well before taking your first bite‼️
▪️Hot Oil Dumplings ($6.25): a MUST! I don’t remember corn being in them last time, but I’m not complaining, lol. The filling’s super juicy, and I love the fiery aromatics on top🥟

I also like their sour plum drink! I would’ve gotten it this time, but they were sold out 🥲

Lemon Chu-hai

Shochu, Lemon Cordial, Soda

If i could drink one cocktail forever it would probably be this, or a whisky highball. Light and refreshing, this version of the lemon chu hai uses a lemon cordial made with oleo saccharum. (other folks will call it a sherbet, but i call it delicious). All you really need is time for this project, and this cordial can be extended to other citrus fruits (though you might have to acid adjust).

45 ml | 1.5 oz shochu

20 ml | .75 oz lemon cordial

2 drops saline solution (20:80)

Soda, to top

Lemon peel, as garnish 

In a highball glass, add ice, shochu, lemon cordial saline solution, and stir to chill. Top with soda, then mix gently to incorporate. Express lemon peel and place on top of the glass.

This place has the BEST Korean fried chicken in Maryland‼️ Run, don’t walk, to @kong.pocha to get your fix. They’re easily the best Korean spot in Columbia, and I can’t recommend them enough. Here’s everything we got: 

▪️Sweet & Spicy Fried Chicken ($31.99): the BEST Korean Fried Chicken, smothered in the yummiest sweet and spicy sauce. Seriously next level and a must try for real‼️
▪️Spicy Rice Cake with Rose Sauce ($17.99): spicy korean cakes in a creamy rose sauce! An exceptionally tasty sauce that’s great if you want something a little less spicy + rice cakes that are cooked to tender perfection, served with fish cakes, and some veggies🤤
▪️Spicy Jokbal ($36.99): spicy stir fried pig’s trotters! Traditionally served with ssam, but they serve theirs with salad and tortilla chips! Extremely flavorful and quite spicy with a nice chewy texture✨
▪️Corn Cheese ($9.99): grilled corn with mozzarella cheese on top. SUPER cheesy and fun to share 😊 A popular KBBQ dish that people love!

Additional notes 📝 
▪️They’re open SUPER late which is great because late night options are limited in HoCo
▪️They have a second location in Baltimore

A huge thanks to the team for hosting us 🥲🫶 

📍9350 Snowden River Parkway UNIT J, Columbia

Can you eat Costco salmon raw?
Yes. And here’s why.
“Sushi grade” isn’t a regulated term in the U.S. — it’s mostly marketing. What is regulated are FDA freezing guidelines for parasite destruction.
Farmed Atlantic salmon is exempt because:� • It’s raised on controlled pellet feed� • It’s not exposed to the wild parasite life cycle� • There are no documented cases of properly farmed Atlantic salmon containing worms
Wild caught can be excellent quality — but for raw consumption it must be frozen to -4°F for 7 days. Most home freezers can’t reliably do that.
That’s why I recommend farmed for beginners.
After 20 years of doing this, I’ve never gotten sick. But remember — while parasite risk is extremely low with farmed salmon, bacterial risk exists with any raw food.
Keep it cold. Buy it fresh. Handle it properly.
And enjoy.

The Statue of Liberty is more than just a giant statue in New York Harbor-it's one of the most powerful symbols in the world. Gifted by France to the United States in 1886, she represents freedom, hope, and new beginnings, especially for immigrants who once saw her as the first sign of America after long, exhausting journeys. The torch she holds stands for enlightenment, while the broken chains at her feet symbolize breaking free from oppression. Over time, the statue has become a reminder that America is built on the idea of opportunity and second chances, even if reality doesn't always live up to the promise.
Whether you see her as history, symbolism, or
 pure icon status, the Statue of Liberty still
watches over the harbor as a silent message of freedom to the world.

The romance anime 🫶🏻

Love Through a Prism (Japanese title: Prism Rondo) is a 20-episode historical romance anime produced by WIT Studio that premiered on Netflix on 15 January 2026. Created and co-written by Yoko Kamio (Boys Over Flowers), the series is set in early 20th-century London and follows a cross-cultural story centered on art and ambition. 
Core Premise & Plot
Setting: Early 1900s London, primarily within the prestigious Saint Thomas Art Academy.
The Protagonist: Lili Ichijoin (voiced by Atsumi Tanezaki), a Japanese exchange student under pressure to top her class in six months or return home to inherit her family's kimono business.
The Rivalry: Lili encounters Kit Church (voiced by Koki Uchiyama), a gifted but emotionally detached aristocratic student. Their shared passion for painting leads to a fierce rivalry that gradually blossoms into romance.
Themes: The story explores the "prism" of art—how characters see and understand each other through their creative visions—while navigating social expectations, gender roles, and the looming shadow of World War I. 
Production Credits
Studio: WIT Studio (Attack on Titan, Spy x Family).
Director: Kazuto Nakazawa (B: The Beginning).
Original Character Designs: Yoko Kamio.
Music: Naoki "naotyu-" Chiba.
Theme Song: "Star Flower" by Chilli Beans.. 
Manga Adaptation
A manga version of the story, illustrated by Maki Minami, began serialization in January 2026 on Shueisha’s Shōnen Jump+ and Manga Mee platforms. It is also available in English on the MANGA Plus app.

Running your knife under water before cutting an onion helps reduce crying because onions release sulfur compounds that turn into an irritating gas when cut. The moisture on the blade helps trap and dilute these compounds, limiting how much reaches your eyes and reducing irritation while chopping.

Placing a bowl of water with lemon slices in the microwave and heating it for about five minutes creates steam that loosens stuck-on odors. The lemon’s natural oils neutralize smells, leaving it fresh. Flattening meat before freezing saves space, stacks neatly, freezes faster, and thaws more evenly and quickly.

F*ck or Suck?

Stuffed Chicken Fries 🍟 

These f*cked but… I think they can be better. 

Dip them in an egg wash and roll them in bread crumbs before air frying. That would take them to where they need to be!

👨‍🍳 Ingredients

Per stick
✅ 80g chicken mince, 2.8oz
✅ 20g light mozzarella, 0.7oz
✅ 10g grated parmesan, 0.35oz
Salt and pepper

🔥 How to make it
Shape into sticks around your mozzarella stick, coat in parmesan or egg and breadcrumbs, air fry at 195 Celsius, 385 Fahrenheit, for 10-15 minutes.

📊 Calories and Macros
Per stick Calories: 214 Protein: 24g Carbs: 1g Fat: 13g

Original Video: @jakecalorie

⇩ Full Recipe 🍚 ⇩

Macros per serving (half the rice cooker):

Protein: 49g
Carbs: 60g
Fat: 14g
Calories: 572

Ingredients per 2 servings (the entire rice cooker):
- 2/3 cup jasmine rice, uncooked
- 3 cups chicken bone broth
- 1/2 cup onion
- 10oz boneless, skinless chicken thigh
- 1 medium-sized carrot
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/2 tbsp minced garlic

Topping:
- 1 medium-sized green onion
- 1 tsp sesame seeds

How to make it yourself:
1. Thinly dice your carrot, green onion, and onion. 
2. Wash your raw rice and place the washed rice into the rice cooker. 
3. Pour in your chicken bone broth and add in your onions, carrots, sesame oil, soy sauce, salt, pepper, minced garlic, and chicken thighs.
4. Set your rice cooker to cook for 1 hour on the porridge or white rice setting and let it cook
5. Once done mix everything together and shred the chicken using a spoon or fork. 
6. Take out half of the porridge from the rice cooker, plate it, top it off with half the green onions and sesame seeds, and enjoy. 

📩 Save this One Pot Rice Cooker DakJuk (Korean Chicken and Rice Porridge) recipe to make for later and if you make it, post it and tag me in it! I’d love to see how you liked the recipe :)

The most comforting & simple healthy Chinese dessert coming your way 🫶🏼

Addressing top comments: 
• Yes this recipe needs to be cow’s milk (it’s the cow protein casein that’s needed to create the chemical reaction) 
• I’ve never tried lactose free / low fat cows milk but my guess is anything more processed is less likely to clump due to alteration in protein structure 
• If using plant-based milk, will probably need to add a clumping agent such as gelatine, starch or agar agar powder to create similar texture 

🫚🥛 姜撞奶 aka “Ginger Smashes Milk” 🫚🥛

Ingredients: (per serve) 
180ml fresh/pasteurised milk 
15g ginger juice (1 tbsp) 
Optional: 1 tbsp sugar 

Steps: 
1. Wash ginger, remove skin, grate well then sieve through to extract juice. (Alternatively, blend ginger then squeeze through cheese cloth)
2. Heat milk to 70-80C (add sugar in here if you’d like) 
3. To a bowl, add 1 tbsp ginger juice then pour milk directly in. 
4. Place a plate on top and leave it to set for 8-10mins. *DO NOT stir/move the bowl during this time. 
5. Enjoy!

Want to know a secret?👀

Your characters feel flat, not because they’re bad… but because they’re underdeveloped.

You’re focusing too much on:
 ⁃  vibes
 ⁃  aesthetics
 ⁃  trendy tropes/archetypes (shadow daddies… I’m looking at you)

When you should be digging into their:
 ⁃  goals & motivations
 ⁃  internal conflicts & wounds
 ⁃  fears, desires, and contradictions
 ⁃  choices that actually drive the plot

If you’ve ever stared at your manuscript and thought:
Why does my MC feel… off?
Why isn’t this scene hitting the way I want it to?
Why does my character feel inconsistent?

It’s because you don’t know who they actually are at their core being.

But once you do, your novel practically writes itself.

Mulled Wine Soju Punch

😗my uncle scoffed and said this tasted  like juice but jokes one sip and he was nonverbal

🎄I love my winter warm spiced wine, glögg, but. When I eat rich foods I want something cold. 

🍷✨Hence my spiced wine punch! My family (even mom dad Leetz who don’t rly drink) liked this and said it reminded them of a korean cinnamon bevvy sujeonggwa ☕️

🍊I used more spice than usual bc spice doesn’t come thru as well if the drink is cold! If u want to add even more spice, leave them in the pitcher. If u want more subtle take them out before adding the orange juice. 
—-

Ingredients;
- 1 bottle of soju (used a citrus flavored one if u want it sweeter) 
- 1 bottle of red wine
- 3 cups of Orange juice
- 3 cinnamons sticks
- 2 star anise 
- 4 cloves

SO, we’re at Hangari today aka my go-to spot for soondoobu in Maryland. I was really bummed when BCD closed, but I think their tofu soup is just as good. They offer lunch and dinner pricing (lunch is $1 less at $16.95) and have 9 options to choose from! Seafood’s probably the most popular, but my fave’s the mushroom, with the beef as a close second 😅

I used to get spicy (it’s actually pretty dang spicy), but I’m trying to be nicer to my tummy so I went with medium. Medium’s still hot though so consider yourself warned, lol 🔥Your stew comes with a hot bowl of rice and a few different kinds of banchan (Korean side dishes) 🇰🇷

It comes out boiling, and as I mention in the video, you want to crack your egg into it while it’s super hot so it’s poached in the broth 🥚 you can also ask them to add the egg for you if you don’t want to crack it at your table.

They have a pretty large menu, but I only really get their soondoobu. If you want to try something else, their kalguksu is pretty good, too 🫶

Have you guys had soondoobu before? LMK by commenting below, Nomsters‼️

🎥: recorded and edited by the amazing @minjesuh 👯‍♀️

Did you know you could HAND MAKE Ichiran Ramen at home in just 20 minutes 😱

Ryuji’s Recipe (Translated): 

 Soup:

- Water: 340ml
- Garlic Powder: 5 pinch
- Pork belly slices: 30g
- Pork fat: 1 tablespoon
- Japanese Sake: 2 tablespoon
- Pork stock powder: 1 tablespoon
- MSG, IMP, GMP blend: 7 pinch
- Gelatin powder: 3/2 teaspoon
*Boil 10 minutes and blend*
- Sugar: 1/4 tablespoon
- Soy sauce: 3/2 tablespoon
- Black pepper: 2 pinch
- Soy Milk: 2 teaspoon
*Blend again*

Spicy Sauce:

- Sesame oil: 1/2 teaspoon
- Gochujang: 1 tablespoon
- Red chilli powder: 1/2 teaspoon
- MSG: 1 pinch
- Water: 1/3 teaspoon

Thank you chef @shunchanjp 👨‍🍳

Tonkatsu in RV kitchen🚌💞Here’s how I make it at home🫶You’ll need:
– Pork loin cutlets
– Salt & pepper
– Flour
– 1 egg (beaten)
– Panko (Japanese breadcrumbs)
– Oil for frying

– UMAMI SAUCE
– Ketchup 

– Shredded cabbage on the side

Lightly score the white muscle fibers on the pork (to keep it from curling). Season both sides evenly with salt and pepper. Dust with flour, dip in beaten egg, then coat with panko. Deep-fry in oil until golden and crispy.
Serve with shredded cabbage and UMAMI SAUCE🍽
Simple, satisfying, and full of love❤️

Traditional Japanese Dresses
Kimono (きもの): A formal, T-shaped, full-length robe, traditionally made of silk, worn for ceremonies and special occasions. The term "kimono" itself means "thing to wear".
Yukata (ゆかた): A casual, unlined cotton robe, simpler and lighter than a kimono. The name means "bathing cloth," and it is most commonly worn during the summer for festivals or as a robe at traditional inns.
Hakama (はかま): A pleated, wide-legged garment resembling a skirt or trousers that is worn over a kimono. It was historically worn by samurai and is now worn for formal occasions like graduation ceremonies and in some martial arts.
Furisode (ふりそで): A highly formal kimono for young, unmarried women, distinguished by its long, "swinging" sleeves. It is often worn for the Coming-of-Age ceremony and other formal social functions.
Uchikake (うちかけ): An elaborate, heavily decorated outer coat worn over a kimono, typically by a bride during a traditional wedding ceremony. It is worn unbelted and is often padded at the hem.
Happi (はっぴ): A simple, short-sleeved cotton coat often worn at festivals or by workers. Happi coats often feature a symbol, crest, or text on the back or lapels to represent a group or event.

🛒 Do you know about Kappabashi, Tokyo’s largest kitchenware street?!

Let me introduce you to Kappabashi Street, Tokyo’s biggest kitchenware district.

It’s about a 10-minute walk from Asakusa, and along this one street, you’ll find around 160 stores—selling everything from everyday kitchen tools to artisan knives, baking gear, coffee equipment, and professional-grade utensils.

Even if you’re just window shopping, it’s 100% worth the stop.

If you’re not sure what souvenirs to bring home, consider grabbing some affordable, well-designed ceramics or chopstick rests—they make great gifts (or keepsakes)

It’s also conveniently located between Asakusa and Ueno, so it fits easily into most Tokyo itineraries.

—————————————————————

If you’re in Japan and you see a radish logo, you need to stop by — this is Shabu-yo, a popular shabu shabu chain.

When you arrive, let the staff know how many are in your party, and they’ll hand you a clipboard with your table number. Their cheapest lunch option is only 1,599 yen per person on weekends, and today we went one step above that, which came with pork belly, chicken, and pork tenderloin.

You can choose between 2 or 4 soup bases. We picked 2: a spicy red kimchi miso (my husband went for “very spicy,” which he said tasted like spicy ketchup), and a basic bonito broth to balance it out.

Ordering is done through an iPad, and the food even arrives on a cat robot, which was really fun. While the broth heats up, you can grab vegetables, udon, rice, and even their house curry from the self-serve section. I tried the curry and was surprised by how flavourful and tender the beef was.

The meat we ordered was fatty, delicious, and even better when dipped into my custom sauce. And for dessert, they have a waffle station and self-serve ice cream with toppings — I got vanilla with chocolate chips and corn flakes, which was surprisingly so good.

Overall, Shabu-yo isn’t the highest quality meat you’ll ever have, but for the price, it really hits the spot. I’d give this a 4/5 and recommend it for anyone looking for an affordable all-you-can-eat in Japan.

This is seriously one of the best appetizers I’ve ever made, and easy to throw together!

🔥 Roasted Tomato Burrata Dip with Garlic Bread Crostinis🍅🧄

Ripe bursted cherry tomatoes, stir in burrata or stracciatella, and scoop it all up with crispy garlic crostinis. A total flavor bomb… have the recipe ready, people WILL ask for it!

🛒You’ll Need:
• cherry tomatoes
• Olive oil
• Salt, pepper, red pepper flakes
• Garlic, parsley, oregano
• Burrata or stracciatella (burrata filling)
• Fresh basil
• Parmesan
• French baguette
• Butter, garlic, salt, parm, parsley (for garlic butter)

🧈 How to Make:
	1.	Add cherry tomatoes to a baking dish and drizzle generously with olive oil (enough to coat the bottom of your dish).
	2.	Season with salt, pepper, red pepper flakes, garlic, parsley, and oregano.
	3.	Bake at 425°F for 20-30 mins until tomatoes burst (you can gently smash them halfway if they need some assistance in the ‘bursting’ process).
	4.	While that bakes, slice a French bread baguette, spread with garlic butter (butter, garlic, salt, parsley, parm), and toast 5–7 mins until golden.
	5.	Once you’re ready to serve, top with parm, fresh basil, and burrata. Stir it all together and serve HOT.

Dip. Scoop. Repeat. This one is next level tasty 🤤  definitely a heavy appetizer, perfect to bring to a party or to serve at your next gathering. 

Everything was purchased at @aldiusa 🧡

Dining in a private tatami room in Kanazawa 

One of the most memorable and unique dining experiences we had while visiting Kanazawa!

We visited @barrierofficial while in Kanazawa and enjoyed beautiful multi-course dinner here in one of their private tatami rooms. The whole experience was unreal, really embodying Japan itself and is something I personally think you wouldn’t want to miss out on because this one meal covers Japanese culture and cuisine all in one go. 

When finding this spot - thanks TikTok 😂 I was already really intrigued by the set up and knew I had to come try! Their whole concept revolves around dashi so naturally each course served is something that is enjoyed with the stock. I loved how everything was served in the box that has multiple levels carrying each course — very Japanese and really felt like we were having a cultural experience at the same time! 

Food wise, flavours were amazing. Loved the food, it didn’t feel overly salty or heavy and we were left very full! ☺️ 

Sitting on a tatami mat and enjoying a dinner like this was definitely a first for me — let’s just say it’s not for the weak. It does get a little uncomfortable sitting cross legged / kneeling but it’s all part of the experience 🥹 the staff here have limited English but they have a translator app handy and we found the communications to be fine. 

Would you try? ☺️ 

📍 Barrier
〰️ 2-14-21 Owaricho, Kanazawa

AiNA THE END x Yuuri - BETTER than the original

AiNA THE END and Yuuri team up for a special acoustic performance of “On The Way (革命道中)”, the second opening theme of Dandadan Season 2. 
Originally written by AiNA and arranged by Shin Sakiura, the full track is known for its explosive energy and raw emotion—but here, stripped down, it reveals an entirely new side.

AiNA, the former BiSH powerhouse celebrated for her husky, emotional vocals, blends perfectly with Yuuri, the singer-songwriter whose rise from Shibuya’s streets to national acclaim has won over countless fans. Together, their voices capture the song’s heart: the struggle to protect what matters most, wrapped in tenderness and love.

Here are some buzzing bee facts!

🐝There are over 4,000 species of native bees in the United States.
🐜Bees are related to the ant.
🤭Bees are the only insect in the world that make food that humans can eat.
🌻Nectar from about 2 million flowers makes 1 pound of honey.
🍯Honey has natural preservatives and bacteria can't grow in it.
😋Honey was found in the tombs in Egypt and it was still edible! 
🌸Bees can smell hundreds of floral varieties and identify pollen from miles away!
🛩A hive of bees will fly 90,000 miles, the equivalent of three orbits around the earth to collect 1 kg of honey.
🌏It only takes about 1 ounce of honey to fuel a bee to fly around the world
🍆The honey bee does not know how to pollinate tomato or eggplant flowers. 
🤰Bees have two stomachs - one stomach for eating and the other is for storing nectar. 
🤓Bees can better identify native plants as food sources than foreign plants.
😃Every 3rd mouthful of food is produced by bees pollinating crops. 
💸Honey bees contribute over $14 billion to the value of U.S. crop production.
🇺🇸U.S. honey bees also produce about $150 million in honey annually.
🗡Male drone bees don't have a stinger.
🤚🏻Swatting at bees causes them to release pheromones that call for more bees. 
💐Bees depend on flowers Spring, Summer, & Fall. When planning your gardens, be sure to include nectar sources for all seasons

Facts from Country Flower Farms article 20 Facts About Bees 🐝

This is the easiest & crispiest Peking Duck you can make in an air fryer that get’s you as close as you can to that legendary crispy skin. You also don’t need to roast an entire duck or blow air into a duck with a bike pump — that’s not a joke, that’s exactly what the traditional recipe calls for.

For the full recipe with step-by-step video guides, macros, and more, check out:

thefoodie.menu/recipes/80-20-peking-duck 👨‍🍳

[Use “DUCK” for access]

Or comment “duck” or “recipe” below for a direct link to the recipe!

Cooking doesn’t need to be hard or complicated. Often, you’re simply looking for a recipe that gets you most of the way there with minimal effort, hence 80/20.

So I did a lot of homework, testing different seasonings, dry times, and cook times — and I think this is probably as close as you can ever get with simply an air fryer. Now to be perfectly clear, this method could never beat the real Peking duck you would find at a restaurant in Beijing — that would be a ridiculous claim, but it does give you remarkably crispy and delicious duck that will definitely satisfy your Peking duck craving.

This historic French restaurant is serving amazing food and an ambiance that will teleport you to the French countryside. This iconic spot originally opened in 1954 and is one of the oldest French restaurants in the area — it’s even said to have served every U.S. president since Harry Truman! The interior is elegant yet cozy and feels like you’re in someone’s home, decorated from floor to ceiling with family heirlooms and murals depicting scenes of the founder’s birthplace in Obernai, France. 

Highly recommend the escargot with garlic herb butter, the beef tenderloin and any of their soufflés! We especially loved the Grand Marnier soufflé.

오사카 디저트 덕후들 사이에서 입소문 난 '비밀 파르페' 집을 다녀왔다. 구글맵에도 등록되지 않은 이곳은 입장부터 주문까지 독특한 방식으로 운영되어, 마치 비밀스러운 퍼즐을 푸는 기분이 들었다.

자리에 앉으면 눈앞에 작은 문 하나가 놓여 있고, 주문한 파르페가 완성되면 문에 불빛이 켜진다. 시즌마다 바뀌는 파르페의 구성과 비주얼은 정말 예술 작품 같았고, 이번에 맛본 파르페는 색감부터 플레이팅까지 완벽했다. 지금까지 오사카에서 먹은 파르페 중 단연 1등.

The Fragrant Flower Blooms with Dignity" tells the story of Rintarō, a gentle student at Chidori High, a boys' school known for lower-class delinquents, and Kaoruko, a girl from the prestigious Kikyo Private Academy Girls' High. Despite the schools' rivalry, Rintarō and Kaoruko form a connection, and their friendship blossoms, eventually leading to romance. The manga explores themes of societal expectations, overcoming prejudice, and finding common ground between seemingly different worlds. 

Here's a more detailed breakdown:

Rival Schools:

Chidori High and Kikyo Private Academy are located next door to each other and have opposing reputations, with Chidori known for its delinquent students and Kikyo for its honorable, high-class girls. 

Rintarō and Kaoruko:

Rintarō, a seemingly tough but kind-hearted student, meets Kaoruko, a student at Kikyo, at his family's bakery. They immediately connect, surprised by each other's kindness and open-mindedness.

Shredded Chipotle Beef 🔥🌶️🥩 

This beef right here is flavor packed and so easy to make. Also GREAT for meal prepping. Use it for tacos, bowls, whatever you want! Very similar to Chipotle's barbacoa!

FULL RECIPE ⬇️

INGREDIENTS
- ~3 lb chuck roast
- Seasonings: Salt, pepper & garlic powder 
- ~1-2 tbsp of ghee
- 2 bay leaves 

(Braising Liquid: Blend all ingredients together) 
- ~1.5-2 cups beef broth 
- ~2-4 chipotles in adobo sauce 
- ~1-2 tbsp of adobo sauce 
- ~2 tbsp of minced garlic 
- ~2 tbsp of apple cidar vinegar 
- Juice of a lime 
- ~ 2 tbsp cumin
- ~ 2 tbsp mexican oregano 
- ~ 1 tbsp onion powder 
- ~ 1 tbsp garlic powder
- ~ 1/2 tbsp salt 
- ~ 1/2 tbsp pepper

Note(s): Measurements are estimated, adjust ingredients to your liking. If you prefer spicier, add extra chipotles to the braising liquid. I personally don't like it to spicy so I only added 3. 

DIRECTIONS 
1.  Prepare the braising liquid and set aside. 
2. Pat the chuck roast dry and season heavily on all sides with salt, pepper and garlic powder. Sear in ghee or oil of choice on a large skillet for 3-4 min per side on medium-high heat.
4. Pour a layer of the liquid in the bottom of the crockpot then add in the chuck roast. Pour the rest of the liquid in and add the bay leaves. Cook on high for 4-6 hours until tender and start to falls apart.
5. Remove and let cool for a few minutes then shred it up. Add some of the broth over top.
6. Serve it with whatever you want & ENJOY! 🙌🏻

The cutest meet and greet 🐧 These penguin encounters let you meet penguins and yes, you can pet them too! You sit in a small group while the penguins get up close and personal and the keepers teach you more about them. With over 100 penguins, the zoo is home to the largest colony of African penguins in North America. It kills me when people visit Virginia and only explore D.C. - and completely miss places like this 😩👇

From wild ponies to otherworldly rock formations, Southwest Virginia is PACKED with hidden gems for hikers and nature lovers.

🪨 McAfee Knob: The most photographed spot on the Appalachian Trail and for good reason. Sunrise here? Unreal.

🐴 Grayson Highlands: Wild ponies, epic balds, and one of the best family-friendly hikes in the state.

💧 Devil’s Bathtub: 500+ steps to a crystal-clear swimming hole that feels like something out of a fairytale.

🌀 Great Channels: Massive sandstone slot canyons right in Virginia. Yep, you read that right. 

🌲 Hungry Mother State Park: A peaceful lake escape with paddleboarding, trails, and toddler-friendly fun.

🌿 Gentry Falls: A lesser-known double waterfall hike near the Tennessee border. Be ready for a lot of creek crossings!

🌄 Sand Cave & White Rocks: A combo hike at the KY border with a cave big enough to picnic in and views that rival out west.

Virginia isn’t just for lovers! It’s for hikers, adventurers, and anyone who wants to experience natural beauty without the crowds.

Save this list for your next road trip and don’t sleep on Southwest VA.

People have traveled here to “take the waters” for centuries, and for good reason. The springs:
💦 bubble from the springs at a temperature of 74.3ºF all year 
💦 flow at a rate of 2000 gallons per minute!!
💦 have an unusually high mineral content which can provide a variety of health benefits to those who come for a soak.

They’re also incredibly historic. They hold the title of being America’s First Spa and were frequented by Native Americans long before George  Washington visited for the first time in 1748. Now more than 55 different spa treatments are available among the spas of Berkeley Springs, and you can also fill your waterbottles with spring water, dip your toes in the outdoor springs & swim in the public pool filled with spring water.

But if you want to visit the Historic Roman Bathhouse shown in this video, here are some logistics for visiting…

📍Berkeley Springs State Park

🎟️ $32 per 30minute soak +$22 if you’d like to have someone else join you

Sweet, earthy, and a touch of tart🍋

Ingredients (makes about 15 slices):
- 6 lemons sliced in half and carved
- 2/3 cup hot water
- 2 packs unflavored gelatin (.25 oz. each)
- 2/3 cup fresh squeezed lemon juice
- 2/3 cup Empress gin
- 1/3 cup lavender simple syrup

Steps:
1. Slice lemons in half then use a spoon to remove flesh. 
2. Place inside of lemons in a bowl and muddle to remove the juice. 
3. Once muddled, strain into a measuring cup to remove any pulp or seeds. Should have about 2/3 cup lemon juice
4. Place carved lemon cups in a baking dish, make sure they are stable and standing
5. Bring water to a boil then turn heat to low
6. Sprinkle unflavored gelatin on top of water and allow to bloom for 3 minutes
7. Whisk gelatin until completely dissolved
8. Remove from heat then whisk in fresh lemon juice, Empress gin, and lavender simple syrup
9. Once combined, pour into lemon cups then place in the fridge for 3-4 hours
10. Once shots are firm, remove from the fridge and place on a cutting board. Slice lemon shots in half, knife touching the Jell-O shot first.
11. Once your shots are perfectly sliced, garnish with a sprinkle of granulated sugar.
12. Serve on a plate with mint and enjoy!

I made [Oyakodon]!
This time I made oyakodon in the same frying pan that I used to cook the meat.

I also tried cooking the meat until it was fragrant before using it.

After eating the same oyakodon for so long, I thought it would be nice to try something like this every once in a while.

However, if you want to make the most of the dashi stock, I personally feel this method is a bit different... or so I thought!

After all, oyakodon cooked in dashi stock after cutting the ingredients gives it a more Japanese flavor and makes the most of the ingredients. I'm going to keep improving it!

ツナとひじきの炊き込みご飯レシピ✨

ツナ缶の旨みとひじきの風味がご飯に染み込み、シンプルながら奥深い味わいに仕上がります♪

◎材料（5人前）
米：2合
ツナ缶（油漬け）：1缶（70g）
にんじん：1/2本
油揚げ：1枚
乾燥ひじき：10g
塩こんぶ：10g
水：適量

◎調味料（A）
醤油：大さじ1
みりん：大さじ1
酒：大さじ1
砂糖：大さじ1/2

◎トッピング
青ねぎ（小口切り）：適量

◎作り方
1.	米を研いで炊飯釜に入れ、30分以上吸水させる。
2.	にんじんは細切り、油揚げは短冊切りにする。
3.	乾燥ひじきは水で戻し、水気を切る。
4.	炊飯釜に（A）の調味料を加え、水を2合目盛りまで入れて軽く混ぜる。
5.	ツナ缶（油ごと）、にんじん、油揚げ、ひじき、塩こんぶをのせ、通常炊飯する。
6.	炊き上がったら全体を混ぜ、お好みで青ねぎをトッピングして完成！✨

◎ポイント
• ツナ缶の油も加えることで、コクのある仕上がりになります。
• お好みでごまをふると、風味がアップしてさらに美味しく！
・動画の乾燥ひじきは水戻し不用のものを使っています！

最後まで読んでいただきありがとうございます🍚！！

Tuna & Hijiki Mixed Rice Recipe ✨

The umami from canned tuna and the rich flavor of hijiki soak into the rice, creating a simple yet deep taste! This easy dish is made in a rice cooker, so give it a try!

Ingredients (Serves 5)

Rice: 2 cups (Japanese measurement)
Canned Tuna (in oil): 1 can (70g)
Carrot: 1/2 piece
Fried Tofu (Abura-age): 1 piece
Dried Hijiki: 10g
Shio Kombu (Salted Kelp Strips): 10g
Water: As needed

Seasoning (A)

Soy Sauce: 1 tbsp
Mirin: 1 tbsp
Sake: 1 tbsp
Sugar: 1/2 tbsp

Topping

Chopped Green Onion: As needed

Instructions
	1.	Rinse the rice and let it soak in water in the rice cooker for at least 30 minutes.
	2.	Julienne the carrot and cut the fried tofu into thin strips.
	3.	Rehydrate the dried hijiki in water and drain well.
	4.	Add all the (A) seasonings into the rice cooker, then add water up to the 2-cup mark and mix lightly.
	5.	Add the canned tuna (with oil), carrot, fried tofu, hijiki, and shio kombu on top. Start the regular cooking cycle.
	6.	Once cooked, mix everything well and top with chopped green onion if desired. Enjoy! ✨

Tips
	•	Adding the tuna oil enhances the richness and flavor.
	•	Sprinkling sesame seeds will boost the aroma and make it even tastier!
	•	In the video, pre-soaked hijiki that doesn’t require rehydration was used.

Thank you for reading until the end! 🍚

Playing Expedition 33 has reignited my love for RPGs, so I thought I’d do a throwback recipe for one of my favourite RPGs. I’d always wanted to try make Sojiro’s curry, and it turns out @atlus_west released an official recipe a few years ago! For real?!

It turned out surprisingly delicious! Recipe below:

Ingredients: 🍛
2 Onions, diced
1 Carrot, grated
½ an Apple, grated
2 cloves of Garlic, minced
2cm piece of Ginger, grated
500g cubed stewing beef
4 tbsp plain flour
2 tsp ground cumin
2 tsp ground coriander
2 tsp ground turmeric
1 tsp ground cardamom
1 tsp ground cinnamon
½ tsp hot chili powder
¼ tsp ground nutmeg
¼ tsp ground clove
¼ tsp black pepper
200ml red wine
600ml beef stock
1 bay leaf
10g chocolate
1 tsp instant coffee
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
75g plain yogurt
1 tbsp honey
10g butter

Finely dice the onion and cook in 1 tbsp vegetable oil over low heat until golden, about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add garlic, grated carrot, and grated apple; cook for 5 more minutes. Set aside.
In another pan, brown the beef over medium heat. Set aside.
Dry-fry the flour until golden, then add spices and cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
In a heavy pot over medium heat, make the stock. Add the garlic mix, onion mix, and beef. Pour in the red wine, reduce heat, cover, and simmer gently for 45 minutes to 1 hour, until beef is tender.
Uncover, then stir in coffee, chocolate, yogurt, honey, and Worcestershire sauce. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, discard bay leaf, and stir in butter. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with rice.

All ingredients by @woolworths_au @colessupermarkets @lindt_au @macro_wholefoods

Hot & Sour Soup | Soups So Good It Can Cure Any Sickness Ep. 2

INGREDIENTS:
- Pork (Optional)
- ½ kg lean pork pork shoulder or tenderloin, sliced into thin strips

Hot & Sour Soup Components:
- 1 cup shiitake mushrooms, chopped
- 1 cup dried wood ear mushrooms/white fungus hydrated and sliced 
- 1 block of firm tofu, cut into thin strips 
- 1 bamboo shoot, julienned
- 1 carrot, julienned 
- 1 large egg, lightly beaten.
- 6-8 cups chicken stock
- 1 tbsp light soy sauce 
- 2 tbsp dark soy sauce 
- 1 tsp sugar
- 1 tsp ground white pepper
- 1 tsp sesame oil 
- 3 tbsp chinese black vinegar
- cornstarch slurry 
- 1-3 dried red chilies (adjusted to spice tolerance)

INSTRUCTIONS:
- Soak dried wood ear mushrooms and white fungus in warm water for about 15 minutes or until soft. Slice the shiitake mushrooms, fungus and wood ears
- Mix pork strips with 1 tbsp water until absorbed, then add a pinch of salt, 2 tsp oil, and 1 tsp cornstarch. Set aside to marinate.
- Slice tofu, bamboo shoots, and carrots into thin strips. Beat the egg and chop scallions for garnish.
- Heat oil in a hot pan/pot and briefly fry dried chilies until fragrant. Add 6–8 cups chicken stock to the same pan/pot and bring to a boil. Add pork, stir to separate strips.
- Add rehydrated mushrooms, lily buds, bamboo shoots, and carrots. Simmer for 2 minutes. Stir in soy sauces, salt, sugar, white pepper, and sesame oil. Let simmer
- Stir in cornstarch slurry (2 tbsp cornstarch + 2 tbsp water) in a thin stream while stirring the soup. Cook until slightly thickened. Add tofu and simmer briefly. Stir soup in one direction and slowly pour in the beaten egg to create ribbons, then give one gentle stir.
- Turn off the heat. Add black vinegar and more white pepper, then taste and adjust for more heat or sourness as desired. Garnish with scallions and serve and enjoy!

#soup #asmrcooking #asmr

Ep 7 | Soy Sauce Test | Road to Michelin 

⭐️ Save this for your next grocery run ⭐️ 

Cooking I found this trick while researching how soy sauce is actually made — and it’s surprisingly simple. 

Just shake the bottle. If bubbles form and linger, that’s a sign of long fermentation and high soybean content. 

Cheaper sauces usually watered down and rushed - seen by bubbles that disappear quickly 

Fun fact: Kikkoman’s original family companies are older than the United States :O 

In Road to Michelin, I research and test cooking techniques to level up my home cooking skills — and share what I learn with you. Got a tip or ingredient you want me to explore next? Just let me know :)

We were pleasantly surprised by this cheap Tokyo all-you-can-drink deal.

Izakaya Terumae in Shimokitazawa is styled after a traditional Japanese bathhouse — but instead of hot water from the taps, you’re pouring drinks. This means you can get your money’s worth quicker, without needing to call the staff each time. 

Disclaimer: Do NOT take your clothes off

There are three nomihodai (all-you-can-drink) options:

¥398 – 1 hour, 9 drinks
¥698 – 1 hour, 25+ drinks and soda refills
¥988 – 1 hour, all drinks including beer

Table charge: ¥500 per person
Come before 6:30 p.m. and selected food items are 50% off.

Heads up: Indoor smoking is allowed, so keep that in mind if you’re sensitive to smoke.

This DC restaurant is serving a Miyazaki-themed tasting menu highlighting all the beloved characters and scenes from the iconic Studio Ghibli anime films. The four or six-course tasting menu can be paired with cocktails or wine, each with a unique twist. So much thought went into every dish and drink, and both the flavors and presentation were absolutely stunning. This is totally a must-do if you’re a Miyazaki fan or if you just love a unique dining experience! It is definitely one of the best and most memorable meals I’ve ever had! 

Our favorite dishes were Haku’s magic onigiri, Kiki’s herring pie and Totoro’s acorn 🌱 For cocktails, we loved the Savoia S.21 and the Soot Gremlin!

🐉 @labohemedc

Gyukatsu Motomura is overhyped 👀
I’ve tried a lot of chain restaurants in Japan, from ramen joints to Gyukatsu spots and this one actually blew me away. No waiting in line. No mid-tier meat. Just juicy beef that actually hits (you can even get the Wagyu set!). did I mention you get unlimited rice, soup, AND salad too? I waited over an hour at Motomura and left unimpressed…this one was better and faster. If Gyukatsu is on your Japan food bucket list, save this and you’ll thank me later. Send this to your Japan foodie partner in crime!

Probably the cheapest all-you-can-drink deal in Tokyo!

This is @tokiwatei_official, a yakiniku spot famous for its self-serve lemon sour tap, RIGHT at your table. Unlimited refills, zero judgement.

At just ¥550 for 60 minutes, it’s dangerously worth it. Need more time? Top up another 30 minutes for ¥330 (one-time extension only).

You can even choose up to 2 flavours from 8 syrups like ume, cassis and honey.

Personally, lemon sour is my go-to drink as it’s light, refreshing, and it pairs perfectly with yakiniku.

They serve up really good beef tongue and have a wide, reasonably priced food menu starting from just ¥350 ranging from chicken, pork, beef.

🍻 0-Second Lemon Sour Sendai Horumon Yakiniku Barbecue Tokiwatei Akihabara (many outlets across Japan)
📍 1 Chome−19, Kanda Sakumacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo (Yamanaka Bldg B1F)
⏰ Mon–Fri: 5pm–11:30pm / Sat: 12pm–11:30pm / Sun: 12pm–10pm

Save this post to look back on when you go! 
📍Ginzanso
This is a traditional Japanese hotel in Ginzan Onsen in Yamagata, Japan. 
It is minutes away from the famous Ginzan Onsen! 

1)  In the room there is a private two hot spring onsen. You can open the windows and see the most magical snow view. Honestly, it’s hard to find places that offer private onsens (usually public), so having two in your room was amazing. The room itself is so beautiful. Their interior is all natural wood, so when you walk in the room it smells like wood. 
Pick up a free yukata they offer and you can eat that around the hotel as well as outside when you walk to Ginzan Onsen.

Pro tip! Take advantage of the free overcoats and snow boots they offer at the hotel because those will keep you warm and help you walk in the deep snow when you walk over to Ginzan Onsen. I made the mistake of wearing my normal shoes and almost slipped many times 🤭

2) They provide breakfast and dinner and it is the most amazing traditional Japanese course. The breakfast is a buffet and the dinner is a multi course dinner. Honestly such healthy and fresh food 💕

Yuanyuan Shen never expected the Sichuan joint she opened in a former office space along Ellicott City’s Route 40 to be ranked among the best Chinese food in the country.

In December, her @pepperhouse.md restaurant was named the 11th best Chinese restaurant nationwide as part of an annual Yelp list calculated by how highly customers rated them and how many reviews they received. The eatery currently has more than 200 reviews and a 4.7 rating.

The announcement brought fanfare to the small spot at 10176 Baltimore National Pike, and has also helped the other small Asian businesses in the development profit from their success.

Staff at neighboring Korean pub Rainpia and Korean barbecue joint Dae Jang Geum, which share Pepper House’s parking lot, say they’ve seen a boost in traffic the last few months, noticeably from people out of state.

🍰🧀Rice cooker Japanese cheesecake recipe @katlieu 

Japanese cheesecakes took me over three years to perfect using the oven and Bain Marie, and the rice cooker one lives up to my egg-spectations 

search Japanese cheesecake in modernasianbaking dot com or check out the recipe in Modern Asian Baking at Home 😘❤️😍 

Be sure to save these tips for a perfect Japanese cheesecake: 

Firm to stiff peak egg whites: you want the whites to be foamy before adding sugar and vinegar or lemon juice can sub cream of tartar

Lumpy batter = lumpy cheesecake remember that!

A little yolk in your egg whites ok, but more than 1/2 a teaspoon and those whites will not whip up

This cake takes at least 2 cycles in a 5.5 cup Zojirushi rice cooker, once Mixed, once Hard

@takaragawa_onsen_osenkaku gave me so much more than a beautiful onsen experience. It brought me to Gunma prefecture. It brought me to @mt.t_by_hoshinoresorts (my favorite mountain that I’ve been to, likely ever). It brought me to new local friends that I’ve been dying to meet. 

Yes, this Onsen is truly incredible and quite a different experience to the typical Onsen I’ve grown accustomed to here in Japan. But the true magic that came from this experience lies in everything that came after I made the choice to chase a pretty picture I saw here on instagram. 

Gunma, I love you dearly. Thank you. 

📍Takaragawa Onsen, Gunma Prefecture
🕰️: 10am - 4pm
💴 ¥1500 per adult ($10)
🎉 Tattoos are OK!!
👫 Coed (Men+Women together, not naked)
💧 100% natural spring water
🪩 Largest Onsen in Japan

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1. Ohayou gozaimasu = Good morning (formal greeting)
2. Konnichiwa = Hello / Good day
3. Konbanwa = Good evening (night greeting)
4. Arigatou gozaimasu = Thank you very much
5. Sumimasen = Excuse me / Apologies
6. Wakarimashita = Got it
7. Wakarimasen = I'm not sure / I don't get it
8. Mou ichido onegaishimasu = One more time, please
9. Hai = Yes
10. Iie = No
11. Onamae wa? = May I know your name?
12. Yoroshiku onegaishimasu = Pleased to meet you
13. Doko desu ka? = Where might it be?
14. Ikura desu ka? = How much does it cost?
15. Onegaishimasu = I'd like that, please
16. Daijoubu desu = I'm okay
17. Eigo wo hanasemasu ka? = Can you speak English?
18. Toire wa doko desu ka? = Where's the restroom?
19. Kore wo kudasai = I'll have this, please
20. Oishii desu = This tastes great
21. Moushiwake arimasen = I sincerely apologize
22. Mata aimashou = Let's meet again
23. Sayounara = Farewell
24. Otsukaresama desu = Great job / You've worked hard
25. Chotto matte kudasai = Just a moment, please
26. Tasukete kudasai = I need help, please
27. Dou iu imi desu ka? = What do you mean by that?
28. Ogenki desu ka? = How have you been?
29. Hajimemashite = It's a pleasure meeting you
30. Odaiji ni = Take care (get well soon)

We’ve arrived at version 2! This portable Spelltable arc can now break down and reassemble using strong magnetic joints! The 3D printed feet have also been turned into dice trays. This allows them to provide more stability while also holding the dice and other small bits you might need for your game.

You can scan the QR code on Spelltable to turn any phone into a wireless webcam for games. If you already have a laptop bag with a computer and a phone inside, then all you need is a flat pack deckbox and this arch to have a complete mobile setup that allows you to play remote commander with a great view of the board.

I designed a holder for the top bar, but I’m finding that nothing is more minimal and practical than just laying your phone on it.

 Any new thoughts for version 3?

Please keep in mind that this is project I’m doing at home that will eventually be made free somewhere online to 3D print. I really don’t want to hear about how dumb I am for not buying a $30 articulating camera arm from Amazon.

Again, if you have a 3D printer art home, you could go to Lowe’s to buy $3 worth of wood and put this together. If that doesn’t seem affordable, fun, and whimsical then I’m asking you guys to keep your comments to yourselves.

Also! If you think this Lightning Bolt mat is sick you can check out @paramintdesigns for this and tons of other top quality mats with legally sourced art.

"If I asked you to name everything you love, how long would it take before you named yourself? Most people hesitate to put themselves on that list, lost in the idea that loving oneself is somehow selfish or undeserved. We’re quick to express love for family, friends, passions, and even material things, but when it comes to ourselves, we often fall silent. It’s as if we forget that we too deserve the same care and compassion we so easily give to others. Learning to love yourself is not just an act of kindness but a necessity. Without it, we rely too heavily on external validation, waiting for others to fill a void only we can fill. So, next time you think of what you love, remember to start with yourself."

Save for later. Most people are still prompting wrong.

OpenAl President Greg Brockman shared the anatomy of a prompt to fix that.

Here’s how each part works.

1. Goal : Clearly define what you need.

2. Return Format: 
Define how the information should be presented back to you. It’s a blueprint for the answer.

3. Warnings : Set guardrails for accuracy.

4. Context :
This is where you provide extra background information that helps the AI tailor the answer to your specific situation or preferences.

A whole village… full of foxes?! 🦊

Yes, Zao Fox Village is a real place, and it’s as magical as it sounds! Located in the mountains of Miyagi Prefecture, this sanctuary is home to over 100 free roaming foxes, and seeing them play in the snow made it feel like I walked into a winter fairy tale. 

They’re super fluffy, photogenic, and full of personality! You can watch them wander around, nap in the snow, and even (safely) feed them from a special area. Just remember, these cuties are still wild animals, so no touching!

📍 How to get there:
🚅 Take the Shinkansen from Tokyo to Shiroishi-Zao Station
🚌 From Shiroishi Station, take a bus (¥1,000) to the village, but be aware it only runs twice a day, so plan ahead!

If you love animals, winter wonderlands, and unique experiences, this place is a must-visit! Who’s adding this to their Japan bucket list? 🦊❄️✨

In 2011, Ronaldo finished the season with 53 goals for Real Madrid, becoming the player who scored the most goals in one season in the club’s history. By winning the Golden Boot, he became the first athlete to win this award in two different championships. Biography: Cristiano Ronaldo was born in Funchal, Madeira, Portugal. Mother - Maria Dolores dos Santos Aveiro, father - José Dinis Aveiro. His parents named him after US President Ronald Reagan because he was his father’s favorite actor. Early career He has been playing soccer since he was three years old, and his love for the sport became evident when he entered elementary school at the age of six. His favorite youth team was Benfica, but later joined their rivals Sporting. At the age of 8, he played for the Andorinha team, where his father was the coach, in 1995, when he was only ten years old, Cristiano Ronaldo’s fame spread throughout Portugal. Two of Madeira’s leading teams, Marítimo and Nacional, are interested in signing him. Maritimo, the bigger team, miss out on a crucial meeting with Andorinha manager Rui Santos, resulting in Ronaldo joining Nacional. After his success at Nacional, Ronaldo went to Lisbon to play for Sporting on a temporary basis for three days and eventually stayed at Sporting

Did you know these sounds?👂🏻(vote in poll)

Isn’t it amazing how babies communicate with sounds?💕Each little noise they make is actually their way of telling you exactly what they need:

1️⃣ Neh = Hungry (mouth makes an “n” shape)
2️⃣ Owh = Sleepy (mouth forms an “o” shape)
3️⃣ Heh = Uncomfortable (humming sound)
4️⃣ Eair = Gas (from the stomach, often followed by pulling legs up)
5️⃣ Eh = Burp (short sound, indicating they need help burping)

It’s incredible how babies use these simple sounds to express their needs before they can even talk. 

The first sound you’ll hear is a soft “neh,” which means they’re hungry and need to eat🍼 When they’re tired, an “owh” lets you know it’s time for sleep 💤 If they’re feeling discomfort from something like a tight diaper or clothing, you’ll hear a “heh” sound. 

The “eair” sound indicates gas, and they may pull their legs up for relief. And when they need help burping, they’ll make a quick “eh” sound. It’s their early way of communicating!🤍

Understanding these little cues can make those early days a bit easier and help you respond to your baby’s needs more confidently👩🏼‍🍼

Credits: @priscilladunstan and @oprah

The Ultimate COMFORT FOOD — My version of an Okonomiyaki! Although it may not be authentic it’s been adapted to suit our family preferences.  This is a type of savory Japanese pancake packed with shredded cabbage, eggs, dashi, flour, and your choice of fillings, all grilled to golden perfection. 

It’s topped with a okonomi (Japanese BBQ) sauce, creamy Kewpie mayo, condiments of your choice, and a sprinkle of bonito flakes (which I ran out of) + seaweed.

It has an irresistible mix of umami, sweetness, and chew in every bite!

Save this recipe, and whip this out in 20 minutes on a weeknight when you’re craving comfort food.

Its so good that your kids won’t even feel like they’re eating vegetables!

INGREDIENTS

Green Cabbage (shredded): half or 500g

Whole Egg: 3 ea

Peas: 50g

Corn kernels: 100g

Plain Flour: 125g

Sugar: 1 tsp

Salt: 1 tsp

Baking Powder: 1 tsp

Dashi Powder: 1 tbsp

METHOD

1) Mix all the ingredients together.

2) Spoon a large ladle of the mix into a pan on low heat and cook for a few seconds.

3) Add a tbsp of water to the pan and cover with a lid to cook for 5 minutes.

4) Flip the fritter and add a tbsp of water. Cover again to cook for 5 minutes.

5) The fritter will be cooked through, but if you want it to be crispy on both sides, do another flip and cook for a couple of minutes.

6) Serve with okonomi sauce (Japanese BBQ sauce), mayonnaise, and any toppings you like. Best enjoyed with rice!

Don’t forget to save this recipe, and hit that follow for daily content that helps you cook better, quicker, and with less stress!

Osaka Kizu Wholesale Market is a large and well-known market located in the Naniwa Ward of Osaka City. It has a long history, dating back to the Edo period, and is one of the key markets supporting the food culture in the Kansai region. The market is particularly famous for its fresh seafood, vegetables, fruits, and meat products.

While the market mainly serves professional chefs and restaurant owners, it is also open to the public during certain hours. Many people visit the market early in the morning to purchase high-quality ingredients, and the atmosphere is lively and bustling. For those interested in fresh, high-grade food products, the market is a popular destination.

Osaka Kizu Wholesale Market is not only a place for professionals but also attracts food enthusiasts and tourists who want to experience the energy and variety of an authentic Japanese market, especially if they visit in the early morning hours.

 For the general public, the best time to visit is from 8:30 to 10:30 a.m., after the vendors have all arrived. The hours of operation of each store vary, and most stores close around 11:00 a.m.

📍 Churaumi Aquarium, Okinawa Japan 🐠🇯🇵

This data is based on total liters of water, not square footage.

Does anyone else feel sadness alongside their wonder at places like aquariums, zoos, etc?

I know a lot of these big animals are “rescues”, but not all are, and regardless, I can’t help but stare at them and feel immense empathy. I wonder if they know they’re meant for more. To be raised in captivity… only to be a spectacle… It breaks my heart. I sat and watched this male whale shark circle the tank for at least 30 minutes. That’s his whole day… meanwhile there’s a whole ocean out there with his kind. He’s beautiful. And I’m so grateful to have experienced his magnitude, but at the same time I wish he were free. 

What are your thoughts on aquariums, zoos, etc?

You’ll find both affordable and rare collectibles here

Have you ever seen such beautiful pancakes? 🥞 We don’t usually get up early, but when there’s sweet food involved then we’re the first out of the hotel in the morning. This place has become so popular that you have to queue before the restaurant opens to get a slot for the day. We got in early at 7:45, but still had a big line in front of us. The place opens its shutters at 08:30 and you can claim your slot, with a deposit of ¥1000 per person 💴

🇯🇵 We got a slot for 10:15 AM and it was so worth it! They hand-make the batter fresh here right in front of the counter, then they fry it to perfection and then stack them up 3 pancakes high. I went for the fresh peach and bergamot cream 🍑, the peaches were so tender and sweet, and Dylan went for the original honey and butter 🍯🧈 - simple but delicious! 

📍 The name of the restaurant is Benizuru and is located in Nishiasakusa. Share this with your bestie 🥞

#tokyo #japan #pancakes

★Casual Japanese Phrases to Use on Your Trip in Japan 👋 
🇯🇵What's your favorite Japanese word? Let me know in the comments!✨

① Wakatta ＝ Got it.
② Iie, kekkoudesu ＝ No, thank you.
③ Douzo ＝ Here you go.
④ Douitashimashite＝ You are welcome.
⑤ Wakarimashita ＝ I understand.
⑥ Arimasen ＝ There is not.
⑦ Iie, daijoubudesu ＝ No, it is okey.
⑧ Gambatte! ＝ Do your best!
⑨ Yokattara ＝ If you like
⑩ Omedetougozaimasu ＝ Congratilations
⑪ Korehanani ＝ What is this?
⑫ Koredeiidesuka？＝ Is it okey? 
⑬ Chottomatttekudasai ＝  Please wait a moment.
⑭ Wakarimasen ＝ I dont know.
⑮ Onamaeha？＝ What is your name?
⑯ Itterasshai ＝ Take care
⑰ Okaerinasai ＝ Welcome back.
⑱ Tasukatta ＝ It helped.
⑲ Naruhodo ＝ I see.
⑳ Doushite ＝ Why?
㉑ Ikuradesuka? ＝ How much is it?
㉒ Kore kudasai ＝ I will take this.
㉔ Korewomitekudasai ＝ Please look at this.
㉕ Soudesune ＝ That is right.

⬇️ Details below 

Enoshima is said to be a place where dragons have lived since ancient times.

According to the legend, The five-headed Dragon fell in love & was tamed by a Goddess.  It’s said the Dragon, turned himself into the hill to watch over the Goddess, and he still keeps watch over Enoshima today.

People clapping hands in front of its statue are rewarded with sparkling lights and the sound of thunder.

Enoshima is a small island off the coast of Kanagawa Prefecture, fairly near Kamakura. It’s connected to the mainland via a bridge, so you can just stroll on over from the train station.

Iwaya Caves are located at the bottom of Enoshima Shrine, and consists of 3 caves, one which is the home to the Dragon 🐉 .

Entrance to the Shrine: Free
Escalators up: 500yen (worth every penny)
Cost to enter the caves: 500yen

🚇Looking for TOKYO METRO stamps to collect? Look no further! The Tokyo Metro Museum in Kasai, Tokyo has all of the subway line stamps that you can collect. 💯

For only 220 yen (1.5 USD), you can enjoy this educational museum (also kid-friendly). If you want to try their stamps, please purchase first at the museum shop a stamp notebook (220 yen). You can collect almost 20 stamps in this museum alone.

The museum is made up of 7 separate exhibit spaces each displaying a different theme. Of the more than 600 items of material on display, in addition to the genuine Tokyo Subway artifacts the museum boasts video search, model train layouts, video displays, poster exhibitions and a hands-on train simulator. (Info from their website)

📍Tokyo Metro Museum
* 6-3-1, Higashi-kasai, Edogawa-ku, Tokyo, 134-0084 
* Beneath the elevated tracks of the Kasai Station, Tozai Line, Tokyo Metro
* 10am - 5pm (last admission 4:30pm)
* Closed days: Mondays (if it is a holiday or a transfer holiday, the next day)

How to visit:
* Get off at Kasai Station on the Tozai Line, Tokyo Metro (note that express trains do not stop at Kasai Station).

Ticket price:
* Adults: 220 yen
* Children: 100 yen

Treat yourself to an entertaining meal at Tokyo’s stealthy ninja restaurant. From the moment you step in the door, you’ll enter a world resembling a ninjas’ hideout, where these ‘sneaky’ warriors serve you food while performing cool theatrics.

The lunch and dinner menus feature multi-course meals that highlight luxurious ingredients such as lobster, baked turban shells and Omi-Sawai wagyu beef from the southern part of Shiga prefecture. Additionally, the restaurant also offers a vegetarian course as well as a special course for children. 

Be sure to make a reservation for this entertaining dining experience at Ninja Tokyo 🥷 #TimeOutTokyo

Both “kudasai” and “onegai shimasu” are translated as “please” in English, so many students have asked me which one to use when ordering food. First of all, “kudasai” and “onegai shimasu” are not exactly the same. When placing a noun before “kudasai,” it is generally used to request things or time. For example, “Mizu o kudasai” (Please give me water) or “Sukoshi jikan o kudasai” (Please give me a little time). It can also be used to request something tangible. But when using it with verbs in the て-form, the usage is different, as it expresses a polite request for an action.

On the other hand, “onegai shimasu” is used more broadly in requests or situations where you’re asking for a favor. For example, when you ask someone to handle a task for you, you might say, “Kore onegai shimasu” (Please take care of this). If you say, “Kore kudasai,” it means “please give this to me,” implying that you want to receive the item. Saying “Kore onegai shimasu” suggests that you want the person to handle the task.

You can also use “onegai shimasu” when asking for something. It’s common to say “onegai shimasu” after finishing an order at a restaurant, when the waitress asks, “Is that everything?” In that case, we don’t say “kudasai” because we are showing humility towards the staff’s service.

Nezu Shrine in spring is a breathtaking place, known for its vibrant azalea garden that blooms between April and May. The shrine’s grounds are filled with thousands of azalea bushes in various shades of pink, red, white, and purple, creating a stunning sea of color.  Walking through a sea of blooming azaleas, surrounded by vibrant colors and traditional red torii gates. it’s like stepping into a fairytale. A must see if you visit Tokto during Spring Time.

Cremia ice cream is a premium Japanese soft-serve ice cream known for its rich, creamy texture and luxurious taste and is something I have to get everyday here in Japan. Made with high-quality Hokkaido milk and fresh cream, it has a higher butterfat content than typical soft-serve, resulting in a velvety smooth consistency. Cremia is often served in a distinctive, buttery langue de chat (cat tongue) cookie cone, which complements the ice cream’s decadence. The combination of the creamy ice cream and the crispy, flavorful cone makes Cremia a sought-after treat and it is my favorite frozen treat of all time over any ice cream or gelato I’ve had. #Cremia #CremiaIceCream #JapanCremia #CremiaJapan #Tokyo #TokyoJapan #Asia #JapaneseIceCream #Softserve #JapaneseSoftServe #BestFrozenTreat #NotTooSweet #BestIceCream #BestSoftServed #VanillaIceCream #MustGetJapan #MustGetTokyo #DessertTokyo #TokyoDessert #Desserts #Dessert #BestJapaneseDessert #HokkaidoMilk #BayAreaFoodie #CookieCone #CrepeCone #BestIceCreamCone

Comment ‘SNACK’ below and we’ll DM you a link to book this Izakaya & Snack Bar Tour in Tokyo 🍻

These snack bars are EXCLUSIVE to the tour, some are members only and not listed on Google Maps, so be sure to book! Experience a night out like a Japanese salaryman with this unique nightlife tour in Shimbashi, Tokyo! 🍻 You’ll explore the heart of Shimbashi, the bustling district known for its bars and entertainment, and explore hidden izakayas and snack bars.

Your evening kicks off at a classic izakaya, where you’ll enjoy a toast with drinks and a variety of skewers and small dishes. 🍢🍶 Get to know your host and fellow participants while joining in on fun quizzes about Japanese drinking culture. ❓🍺

Afterward, you’ll stroll through the lively night streets to a retro snack bar. There, “Mama-san” will greet you in a nostalgic setting filled with old-school decor and music. 🎶 Enjoy karaoke, games, and three drinks with snacks that are a delightful surprise! 🍹🎤

At the end of the night, you’ll receive a special custom souvenir to remember your adventure. 🎁 This tour is perfect for a fun night out, offering a taste of authentic Japanese nightlife away from the usual tourist spots. 🍻🎉

The Aman Tokyo: The brand’s first property in an urban city—and often regarded as the #1 hotel in Japan. 

Perched atop the top six floors of the Otemachi Tower, it’s almost hard to believe you’re in the heart of Tokyo’s financial district. The concept was to recreate the tranquility of a traditional Japanese home… right in the middle of the city. It’s works, but in our eyes, we preferred the original formula.

Aman started as the ultimate insider’s secret—a collection of hidden sanctuaries in remote, hard-to-reach locations. Maybe that magic can be captured in an urban setting—but we’re not quite convinced.

Here’s the question—do Aman’s city hotels measure up to their remote hideaways, or should they have stuck to the their brand roots? Let us know your thoughts below!

Conbini phrases🔽

あたためますか？
Do you want to warm up?

おはし　おつけしますか？
May I add chopsticks?

おしぼり　おつけしますか？
May I add wet tissue?

ポイントカードはおもちですか？
Do you have point card?

ふくろは　ごいりようですか？
Do you need a bag?

レシートは　ごいりようですか？
Do you need a receipt?

To answer these questions, just remember these two phrases!

おねがいします/だいじょうぶです
Yes, please.  No thank you.

16 Course Sushi Omakase 🍣

📍Sushi Itsumi

There are so many omakase options in Japan so it can be hard to find ones that are really worth it… Especially when they can be super expensive! 
Sushi Itsumi is a known and loved restaurant by locals because they offer classic traditional Edomae Sushi for affordable prices! 
This 16 course omakase was $80USD and it’ll leave you full and satisfied! 
Bookings are highly recommended as they often don’t take walk-ins!

👉🏼 you can choose 90 mins, 3 hours or All day
👉🏼 you can leave and come back if you opt for the all day one 
👉🏼 there are adult, children and student rates
👉🏼 they actually have more than 5 floors but they are not included in the spocha unlimited pricing :) 

For more details go to their website!
✔️Search Round 1 stadium (there’s a few in Japan) 
✔️ look for Spocha details and prices

BEST fluffy pancakes in Tokyo!

12 DAYS OF TOKYO - DAY 1

Follow @thefoodinbox for more Japan food and travel 🍜

This place is so popular that you usually need to arrive at 8am for a reservation… but we got lucky and snagged a last minute walk in spot 😎

Now they’re known for their fluffy pancakes which they make right in front of you’re eyes, we started with the seasonal strawberry stack and this was epic, super sweet and delicious 😍

but the star of the show was definitely the bacon and egg with hollandaise sauce, this was honestly LIFE CHANGING - it was so good!

📍 Benizuru, Asakusa @benizuru_homemade_pancake 

#benizuru #asakusafood #tokyofood #fluffypancakes #benizurupancakes

Yes, that’s beef sushi 🍣🥩

I recently had the most wonderful meal at Oniku Karyu in Ginza here in Tokyo with the help of @japanbyfood 🗼

At this Michelin star restaurant, you can to enjoy a multiple course omakase kaiseki meal featuring A4 and A5 grade kuroge wagyu beef hand-selected by the chef 🧑‍🍳 

I am not exaggerating when I say I had no idea beef could taste like this 🤩 It transcended the boundaries of what I thought was meat and became something so incredibly delicious and loaded with umami it’s shocking 😳 

This was my first time at an omakase because I actually don’t eat seafood 😖 But!!!! Even though there was seafood on the menu, they were able to accommodate and switched out the dishes for something else 🥺 So rare in Japan 🤧

👩‍💻 Book this meal for yourself on the byFood platform! Link is in my bio

📍草津温泉 群馬 / Kusatsu Onsen in Gunma

日本三名泉の1つである草津温泉の自然湧出量は日本一を誇り、1日にドラム缶約23万本分もの温泉が湧き出しています♨️ 湯畑の絶景が直近で拝めたり、お湯を冷ますための湯もみショーを楽しめたり、温泉街では食べ歩きを楽しめるのも魅力的！貴方も草津温泉で癒されてみてはいかがですか？

Kusatsu Onsen, one of Japan's 3 most famous hot springs, boasts the largest natural spring water output in Japan, with about 230,000 drums' worth of hot spring water gushing out every day. You can enjoy the spectacular view of the Yubatake hot springs up close, a hot spring kneading show to cool the water, and food tours in the hot spring town! Why not try relaxing in Kusatsu Onsen? ♨️

🎢 Things To Do: Japan | Joypolis 🎢
📍Tokyo Beach
💰Admission + Unlimited Rides: $35
+ must present your passport for a discount
⏰11am-7pm or 8pm

Since returning from Japan, I keep telling people they’re missing out on a huge hidden gem called Joypolis! I could literally rave about this place for hours. It’s an indoor entertainment center with several floors - each consisting of interactive games & even mini roller coasters!

It’s located inside of a mall, but don’t let that confuse you on the size because this place is HUGE! The top floor is my favorite where they have several interactive rooms & mazes for you to explore. Including 1 that’s “The Ring” themed! They put you in a casket and you have to ‘escape’ - which is CRAZY! They have a few horror themed mazes & some local ot the Japanese culture such as Attack on Titan (one of the only anime’s I actually know). You could literally spend all day here & we honestly were so sad we had to leave, but we stayed until they closed up the location!

They also have a mini roller coaster where you play games during the ride (4 players) and once the player wins, you spin around on a mini coaster! I can’t even put into words how amazing this location was & I can’t wait for you to experience it yourself so make sure to add this bucket list destination to your Japan itinerary!

<The 16 BEST BREWERIES & BARS in TOKYO>
◎SHIBUYA & SHINJUKU◎
🍺 Y.Y.G Brewery & Beer Kitchen
  Unique craft beers brewed on-site, paired with gourmet food.

🍺 BEER KOBO SHINJUKU
  Cozy atmosphere with a variety of fresh, locally brewed beers.

🍺 YEBISU BREWERY TOKYO
  Offers an immersive brewery tour with tastings of their premium beers.

🍺 VECTOR BREWING
  Known for its experimental brews and vibrant beer community.

🍺 OUR DAYs Brewery & clubhouse
  Combines a relaxed vibe with a great selection of craft beers.

🍺 FIVE TREES
  Small, hidden gem with a strong focus on quality over quantity.

◎UENO & ASAKUSA◎
🍺 ASAKUSA BEER KOBO
  Offers craft beers with a view of traditional Asakusa.

🍺 Yanaka Beer Hall
  Laid-back atmosphere in the historic Yanaka district.

🍺 TOKYO BUTCHERS with OKACHI Beer Lab
  Excellent craft beers paired with high-quality meats.

🍺 OKEI BREWERY
  Known for its creative and flavorful brews.

🍺 Folkways Brewing
  A blend of modern brewing techniques and traditional recipes.

🍺 Kichi Brewing
  A hidden spot with a rotating selection of fresh beers.

🍺 Shared Brewery
  Community-oriented with a focus on collaboration and innovation.

◎SUBURBS of TOKYO◎
🍺 TDM 1874 Brewery
  Historical brewery with a deep connection to local culture.

🍺 Suntory Musashino Brewery
  Offers a fascinating tour of one of Japan’s most famous beer brands.

🍺 ISANA Brewing Brewery & Roastery
  Unique combination of craft beer and specialty coffee.

All-you-can-eat won on my latest story poll so here goes!!! Featuring one of @nom_digital’s wonderful social media clients today, @meatupkoreanbbq 🫶. I think they have the strongest AYCE KBBQ in MD so if you’re looking for an unlimited meal, definitely check them out.

They’re located in College Park, and their interior’s realllllly cool!  They were going for a cyberpunk theme, and they nailed it IMO. For an AYCE spot, I’d say their meat is standout. I especially like their beef brisket, spicy pork bulgogi, and thick cut pork belly🤤

To start, pricing’s as follows:

▪️Weekday Lunch: $21.99pp
▪️Dinner + Weekend: $33.99pp
▪️Kids under 5 eat free, and ages 5-12 is 1/2 off

Additional notes 📝
▪️They offer their entire ramen bar menu (AYCE) for $3.50 as an add on - this includes korean stews (like kimchi jjigae & soon tofu), various apps, Korean fried chicken, bentos and more.
▪️There’s a buffet bar (with veggies, some hot dishes, sauces, and more), and they also offer instagrammable party drinks, boba, desserts, and more.
▪️They take reservations for groups larger than 6 but otherwise it’s first come first serve. 
▪️They also have Kpop playing on the TVs so it’s a fun vibe
▪️Certain proteins are only available for dinner (check their IG menu highlight for more info)
▪️FYI, you cook the meat yourself

Just because travel credit cards have higher annual fees does NOT mean that there aren’t ways to keep the card for FREE or get a bonus offer every year to spend on future travel!

How?

Retention offers are the best way to waive your travel cards’ annual fees or receive a bonus amount of points to offset the cost of keeping the card ✈️💳

Need help with the script? Watch this video to find out what to say and how to respond to get the best available offer for your card!

TIPS TO GET THE BEST RETENTION OFFER FOR YOU:

1️⃣ Call the phone # on the back of your card or use the online chat function and say that you are CONSIDERING canceling your card 

2️⃣ If saying that the annual fee is too expensive for you is not enough, be sure to mention that you already receive the benefits from ANOTHER card with a LOWER annual fee

3️⃣ Make sure to ask about ALL available offers - the first one they offer may not be the best one available to you

4️⃣ If you don’t like the offers available to you, you can try again on a different day - offers change daily, so it never hurts to wait and try another time 

5️⃣ Offers also depend on how long you’ve held the card as well as your spending habits

💳 Try out this script next time you get charged your annual fee and follow @pointstopointtravel for more credit card tips and strategies 💳

My hubby Drew, who likes drinking carbonated water, discovered years ago that a Soda Stream is hands-down the easiest, cheapest and most environmentally-friendly way to have bubbly water any time you want it. So when his doctor prescribed gin and tonics (for his leg cramps - well, the tonic anyway), he found another great way to put the Soda Stream to work. (Fact is, you can buy syrups to make all KINDS of soda at home.) His other cool trick: he puts some lemon, lime or orange rind into the bottle BEFORE he carbonates it - the pressure from the Soda Stream extracts the flavor to make a delicious sparkling soda. Another great Drewster win-win-win. Cheers! #ginandtonic #cocktailhour #drewandamyshow #plasticfree

Day 53: Do you think a cashback credit card can rise above the Team Cashback tier? Well I think the Fidelity Rewards create card not only rises above, but reaches to the A-tier level. Why? It looks like a normal 2% no annual fee credit card that doesn't earn any points or miles.

But unlike the competitors, this card only earns 2% when you put it into a Fidelity (investment) account, which changes the whole dynamic and behavior of rewards. Instead of treating cashback as free money where you'll spend more or as miles to hoard for a later trip, investing the cashback grows your money from normal spend.

So if you're not in the travel game and you don't need the cash back immediately, this is a really solid card too keep on hand.

Congrats to @fidelity for having such an underrated credit card.
